heading: Outsourcing facilities
label: 353b Outsourcing facilities
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 503B of , was renumbered section 503C by , , , and transferred to .act June 25, 1938, ch. 675Pub. L. 113–54, § 102(a)(1)127 Stat. 587section 353c of this titleNov. 27, 2013
amendments: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 503B of , was renumbered section 503C by , , , and transferred to .act June 25, 1938, ch. 675Pub. L. 113–54, § 102(a)(1)127 Stat. 587section 353c of this titleNov. 27, 2013

children.heading: Registration and reporting
children.label: 1 Registration and reporting
children.text: The drug is compounded in an outsourcing facility that is in compliance with the requirements of subsection (b).

children.label: I
children.text: publishing a notice in the Federal Register proposing bulk drug substances to be included on the list, including the rationale for such proposal;

children.label: II
children.text: providing a period of not less than 60 calendar days for comment on the notice; and

children.label: III
children.text: publishing a notice in the Federal Register designating bulk drug substances for inclusion on the list; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the drug compounded from such bulk drug substance appears on the drug shortage list in effect under at the time of compounding, distribution, and dispensing;section 356e of this title

children.label: B
children.text: if an applicable monograph exists under the United States Pharmacopeia, the National Formulary, or another compendium or pharmacopeia recognized by the Secretary for purposes of this paragraph, the bulk drug substances each comply with the monograph;

children.label: C
children.text: the bulk drug substances are each manufactured by an establishment that is registered under (including a foreign establishment that is registered under ); andsection 360 of this titlesection 360(i) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: the bulk drug substances are each accompanied by a valid certificate of analysis.

children.heading: Ingredients (other than bulk drug substances)
children.label: 3 Ingredients (other than bulk drug substances)
children.text: If any ingredients (other than bulk drug substances) are used in compounding the drug, such ingredients comply with the standards of the applicable United States Pharmacopeia or National Formulary monograph, if such monograph exists, or of another compendium or pharmacopeia recognized by the Secretary for purposes of this paragraph if any.

children.heading: Drugs withdrawn or removed because unsafe or not effective
children.label: 4 Drugs withdrawn or removed because unsafe or not effective
children.text: The drug does not appear on a list published by the Secretary of drugs that have been withdrawn or removed from the market because such drugs or components of such drugs have been found to be unsafe or not effective.

children.heading: Essentially a copy of an approved drug
children.label: 5 Essentially a copy of an approved drug
children.text: The drug is not essentially a copy of one or more approved drugs.

children.heading: Drugs presenting demonstrable difficulties for compounding
children.label: 6 Drugs presenting demonstrable difficulties for compounding
A
children.text: is not identified (directly or as part of a category of drugs) on a list published by the Secretary, through the process described in subsection (c), of drugs or categories of drugs that present demonstrable difficulties for compounding that are reasonably likely to lead to an adverse effect on the safety or effectiveness of the drug or category of drugs, taking into account the risks and benefits to patients; or

children.label: B
children.text: is compounded in accordance with all applicable conditions identified on the list described in subparagraph (A) as conditions that are necessary to prevent the drug or category of drugs from presenting the demonstrable difficulties described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Elements to assure safe use
children.label: 7 Elements to assure safe use
children.text: In the case of a drug that is compounded from a drug that is the subject of a risk evaluation and mitigation strategy approved with elements to assure safe use pursuant to , or from a bulk drug substance that is a component of such drug, the outsourcing facility demonstrates to the Secretary prior to beginning compounding that such facility will utilize controls comparable to the controls applicable under the relevant risk evaluation and mitigation strategy.section 355–1 of this title

children.heading: Prohibition on wholesaling
children.label: 8 Prohibition on wholesaling
children.text: The drug will not be sold or transferred by an entity other than the outsourcing facility that compounded such drug. This paragraph does not prohibit administration of a drug in a health care setting or dispensing a drug pursuant to a prescription executed in accordance with .section 353(b)(1) of this title

children.heading: Fees
children.label: 9 Fees
children.text: The drug is compounded in an outsourcing facility that has paid all fees owed by such facility pursuant to .section 379j–62 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the statement “This is a compounded drug.” or a reasonable comparable alternative statement (as specified by the Secretary) that prominently identifies the drug as a compounded drug;

children.label: ii
children.text: the name, address, and phone number of the applicable outsourcing facility; and

children.label: I
children.text: the lot or batch number;

children.label: II
children.text: the established name of the drug;

children.label: III
children.text: the dosage form and strength;

children.label: IV
children.text: the statement of quantity or volume, as appropriate;

children.label: V
children.text: the date that the drug was compounded;

children.label: VI
children.text: the expiration date;

children.label: VII
children.text: storage and handling instructions;

children.label: VIII
children.text: the National Drug Code number, if available;

children.label: IX
children.text: the statement “Not for resale”, and, if the drug is dispensed or distributed other than pursuant to a prescription for an individual identified patient, the statement “Office Use Only”; and

children.label: X
children.text: subject to subparagraph (B)(i), a list of active and inactive ingredients, identified by established name and the quantity or proportion of each ingredient.

children.label: i
children.text: the information described under subparagraph (A)(iii)(X), if there is not space on the label for such information;

children.label: ii
children.text: the following information to facilitate adverse event reporting: www.fda.gov/medwatch and 1–800–FDA–1088 (or any successor Internet Web site or phone number); and

children.label: iii
children.text: directions for use, including, as appropriate, dosage and administration.

children.heading: Additional information
children.label: C Additional information
children.text: The label and labeling of the drug shall include any other information as determined necessary and specified in regulations promulgated by the Secretary.

children.heading: Outsourcing facility requirement
children.label: 11 Outsourcing facility requirement
children.text: The drug is compounded in an outsourcing facility in which the compounding of drugs occurs only in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Registration of outsourcing facilities and reporting of drugs
children.label: b Registration of outsourcing facilities and reporting of drugs
i
children.text: shall register with the Secretary its name, place of business, and unique facility identifier (which shall conform to the requirements for the unique facility identifier established under ), and a point of contact email address; andsection 360 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: shall indicate whether the outsourcing facility intends to compound a drug that appears on the list in effect under during the subsequent calendar year.section 356e of this title

children.heading: Availability of registration for inspection; list
children.label: B Availability of registration for inspection; list

children.heading: Registrations
children.label: i Registrations
children.text: The Secretary shall make available for inspection, to any person so requesting, any registration filed pursuant to this paragraph.

children.heading: List
children.label: ii List
children.text: The Secretary shall make available on the public Internet Web site of the Food and Drug Administration a list of the name of each facility registered under this subsection as an outsourcing facility, the State in which each such facility is located, whether the facility compounds from bulk drug substances, and whether any such compounding from bulk drug substances is for sterile or nonsterile drugs.

children.label: i
children.text: identifying the drugs compounded by such outsourcing facility during the previous 6-month period; and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to each drug identified under clause (i), providing the active ingredient, the source of such active ingredient, the National Drug Code number of the source drug or bulk active ingredient, if available, the strength of the active ingredient per unit, the dosage form and route of administration, the package description, the number of individual units produced, and the National Drug Code number of the final product, if assigned.

children.heading: Form
children.label: B Form
children.text: Each report under subparagraph (A) shall be prepared in such form and manner as the Secretary may prescribe by regulation or guidance.

children.heading: Confidentiality
children.label: C Confidentiality
children.text: Reports submitted under this paragraph shall be exempt from inspection under paragraph (1)(B)(i), unless the Secretary finds that such an exemption would be inconsistent with the protection of the public health.

children.heading: Electronic registration and reporting
children.label: 3 Electronic registration and reporting
children.text: Registrations and drug reporting under this subsection (including the submission of updated information) shall be submitted to the Secretary by electronic means unless the Secretary grants a request for waiver of such requirement because use of electronic means is not reasonable for the person requesting waiver.

children.label: i
children.text: shall be subject to inspection pursuant to ; andsection 374 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: shall not be eligible for the exemption under .section 374(a)(2)(A) of this title

children.heading: Risk-based schedule
children.label: B Risk-based schedule
children.text: The Secretary, acting through one or more officers or employees duly designated by the Secretary, shall inspect outsourcing facilities in accordance with a risk-based schedule established by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: The compliance history of the outsourcing facility.

children.label: ii
children.text: The record, history, and nature of recalls linked to the outsourcing facility.

children.label: iii
children.text: The inherent risk of the drugs compounded at the outsourcing facility.

children.label: iv
children.text: The inspection frequency and history of the outsourcing facility, including whether the outsourcing facility has been inspected pursuant to within the last 4 years.section 374 of this title

children.label: v
children.text: Whether the outsourcing facility has registered under this paragraph as an entity that intends to compound a drug that appears on the list in effect under .section 356e of this title

children.label: vi
children.text: Any other criteria deemed necessary and appropriate by the Secretary for purposes of allocating inspection resources.

children.heading: Adverse event reporting
children.label: 5 Adverse event reporting
children.text: Outsourcing facilities shall submit adverse event reports to the Secretary in accordance with the content and format requirements established through guidance or regulation under section 310.305 of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulations).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall implement the list described in subsection (a)(6) through regulations.

children.heading: Advisory committee on compounding
children.label: 2 Advisory committee on compounding
children.text: Before issuing regulations to implement subsection (a)(6), the Secretary shall convene and consult an advisory committee on compounding. The advisory committee shall include representatives from the National Association of Boards of Pharmacy, the United States Pharmacopeia, pharmacists with current experience and expertise in compounding, physicians with background and knowledge in compounding, and patient and public health advocacy organizations.

children.label: i
children.text: publishing a notice of such substances, drugs, categories of drugs, or conditions proposed for designation, including the rationale for such designation, in the Federal Register;

children.label: ii
children.text: providing a period of not less than 60 calendar days for comment on the notice; and

children.label: iii
children.text: publishing a notice in the Federal Register designating such drugs, categories of drugs, or conditions.

children.label: i
children.text: the date that is 5 years after ; orNovember 27, 2013

children.label: ii
children.text: the effective date of the final regulations issued to implement subsection (a)(6).

children.heading: Updates
children.label: 4 Updates
children.text: The Secretary shall review, and update as necessary, the regulations containing the lists of drugs, categories of drugs, or conditions described in subsection (a)(6) regularly, but not less than once every 4 years. Nothing in the previous sentence prohibits submissions to the Secretary, before or during any 4-year period described in such sentence, requesting updates to such lists.

children.heading: Definitions2So in original. Two subsecs. (d) have been enacted.2
children.label: d Definitions2So in original. Two subsecs. (d) have been enacted.2

children.text: The term “compounding” includes the combining, admixing, mixing, diluting, pooling, reconstituting, or otherwise altering of a drug or bulk drug substance to create a drug.

children.label: A
children.text: a drug that is identical or nearly identical to an approved drug, or a marketed drug not subject to and not subject to approval in an application submitted under , unless, in the case of an approved drug, the drug appears on the drug shortage list in effect under at the time of compounding, distribution, and dispensing; orsection 353(b) of this titlesection 355 of this titlesection 356e of this title

children.label: B
children.text: a drug, a component of which is a bulk drug substance that is a component of an approved drug or a marketed drug that is not subject to and not subject to approval in an application submitted under , unless there is a change that produces for an individual patient a clinical difference, as determined by the prescribing practitioner, between the compounded drug and the comparable approved drug.section 353(b) of this titlesection 355 of this title

The term “approved drug” means a drug that is approved under and does not appear on the list described in subsection (a)(4) of drugs that have been withdrawn or removed from the market because such drugs or components of such drugs have been found to be unsafe or not effective.section 355 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: is engaged in the compounding of sterile drugs;

children.label: ii
children.text: has elected to register as an outsourcing facility; and

children.label: iii
children.text: complies with all of the requirements of this section.

children.label: B
children.text: An outsourcing facility is not required to be a licensed pharmacy.

children.label: C
children.text: An outsourcing facility may or may not obtain prescriptions for identified individual patients.

The term “sterile drug” means a drug that is intended for parenteral administration, an ophthalmic or oral inhalation drug in aqueous format, or a drug that is required to be sterile under Federal or State law.

children.heading: Obligation to pay fees2
children.label: d Obligation to pay fees2
children.text: Payment of the fee under , as described in subsection (a)(9), shall not relieve an outsourcing facility that is licensed as a pharmacy in any State that requires pharmacy licensing fees of its obligation to pay such State fees.section 379j–62 of this title
